thesis: Constellation Energy is the largest carbon-free energy producer in the U.S., operating the nation's leading nuclear fleet. The company is a primary beneficiary of AI data center power demand, with multi-gigawatt PPAs signed with hyperscalers. The transformational $16.4B Calpine acquisition expands the fleet to ~55 GW and is expected to be 20% accretive to 2026 non-GAAP EPS. Stock trades ~30% below 52-week highs, offering attractive entry point.

drivers: Data center PPA volumes accelerate to 5+ GW
Power prices rise 15–20% on AI-driven demand

risks: Regulatory delays on nuclear license extensions
Rising interest rates increase refinancing costs

base_case.assumptions: Calpine integration proceeds on plan; 2–3 GW of new data center PPAs signed; nuclear fleet performs at historical norms; power prices stable; EPS reaches $14–16 by 2027

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 Earnings — Calpine integration update, data center PPA announcements
2026 Annual Guidance Update — Full-year outlook including Calpine contribution
NRC License Extension Rulings — Key plant license renewals

key_risks: Regulatory and political risk — NRC oversight, safety incidents, license revocation
Power price volatility — sensitivity to natural gas prices, weather, grid demand
Calpine integration risk — $16.4B acquisition; largest in company history
Nuclear waste and decommissioning liabilities — long-term off-balance-sheet obligations
Competition from renewables + storage — falling costs of solar, wind, battery storage
